title: The Horizon Interrogation

features.name: a circuit-chip holding beach questions
features.desc-on-touch: A black IC block with pin-dot legs, etched with questions that dissolve at the margins. Each question is a coordinate where sand and light stop becoming separate things.

features.name: a tower where reflection becomes question
features.desc-on-touch: A stacked structure of light-marks on the board, reading coordinates outward. What is the boundary between knowing and reflecting? The archive cannot quite hold both parts of the question at once.

features.name: a glyph-plaque of horizon coordinates
features.desc-on-touch: Unreadable writing, but you feel the horizon's longing in it. A place where every point of sight converges. A manuscript that learned to dissolve at its own edges.

inscription: the horizon asks questions whose answers sound like more questions

file.name: Manuscript_Fragment_Beach_Library_Corrupted.txt
file.note: A text that learned to scatter. Each word a grain of sand. Each sentence a moment of looking toward something that was always receding.

exits.label: back to the shelf where reading begins
toward the horizon's answer, which moves away as you approach
deeper into the sand where books become beach
